title: cerastium pumilum subsp. fallax
intro: Cerastium pumilum subsp. fallax, also known as the 'Mountain Snow', is a low-growing, evergreen perennial plant native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its ability to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cerastium pumilum subsp. fallax include its potential to alleviate fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as a diuretic to treat urinary issues. The herbal preparation of the plant typically involves drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
